_template_metadata.purpose: Reusable template for the 15 Wave-2 Landmark FL counties (Lee, Escambia, St. Johns, Clay, Hernando, Bay, Martin, Indian River, Citrus, Flagler, Monroe, Walton, Wakulla, Levy, Okeechobee). Copy to <county>.json, fill in the FIVE _TODO_ markers below, then add the county_id to counties/registry.py with platform='landmark'. Live-probe to confirm the recaptcha_site_key — some Landmark tenants share the Palm-Beach sitekey, others mint their own.
validation_checklist: 1. curl_cffi.Session(impersonate='chrome120').get(base_url) returns 200 with 'Landmark Web Official Records' in title (Lee may need a different fingerprint behind Akamai)
3. GET /search/index?theme=.blue&section=searchCriteriaName returns the search form (~2-3 MB HTML)
4. POST /Search/ShowCaptcha returns 'True' on cold session (means recaptcha_v2 is enforced)
5. recaptcha_site_key matches the data-sitekey on .recaptchasection-Name in the search form

notes: Landmark Web Official Records platform — same engine as fl_palm_beach. Adapter at src/titlepro/search/recorder/counties/adapters/landmark_adapter.py. See palm_beach.json for a live-validated example. Wave-2 build order (per FL_Implementation_Plan.md): Lee (#8) -> Escambia (#21) -> St. Johns (#22) -> Clay (#25) -> Hernando (#27) -> Bay (#28) -> Martin (#30) -> Indian River (#31) -> Citrus (#33) -> Flagler (#34) -> Monroe (#38) -> Walton (#42) -> Wakulla (#46) -> Levy (#47) -> Okeechobee (#48). Lee sits behind Akamai (bazadebezolkohpepadr) so 'safari17_2_ios' may be needed as impersonate_profile.
